Dick Van Dyke stars as the medical sleuth.(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV star is caught on camera fleeing the scene of her former husband's murder, but Dr Sloan suspects she may have been framed. Guest starring Morgan Fairchild.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Blushing Pearls

programme.sub-title: The Case of Paul Drake's Dilemma

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. The victim had been reported missing since she was evacuated before the blast at NCIS headquarters. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team accepts a job repairing a turbine on a wind farm, but things take a critical turn when Paige and Happy are stranded in a faulty gondola that could plummet at any moment.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and the team defy the orders of a foreign government as they try to retrieve an asteroid, which is thought to containing DNA strands that could prove deadly if exposed.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jo Masters is assigned to look after a recently released child-killer who has jeopardised her new identity by disclosing it to a former acquaintance. Roger gallantly offers to let penniless Russian prostitute Anka Markova stay with him, while Sheelagh is disturbed to learn assault victim Zoe Dowman won't make a statement because she's afraid of ex-boyfriend Gabriel Kent. Pauline Quirke guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sheelagh finally decides to accept Gabriel's marriage proposal, and Smithy patches up his friendship with Gina, who is feeling more amenable to his overtures now she's been given the cancer all-clear. Meanwhile, Roger agrees to investigate an assault on his estranged wife Sandra's new boyfriend Rick - whose reticence to discuss the incident is soon explained by the discovery that he's a crook.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Reg organises a half-centenary celebration of Sun Hill, attended by dignitaries including former DCS Charles Barnett. On a less happy note, Amanda Prosser's insensitive treatment of a bereaved father provokes a hostage crisis, and the real Gabriel Kent turns up out of the blue, spelling big trouble for the unscrupulous copper who has assumed his identity.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. In an episode originally broadcast live, distraught dad Jeff Clarke holds the Sun Hill stalwarts at gunpoint, prompting a desperate response from Dan Casper - who ends up getting shot, along with the real Gabriel Kent. Fortunately, Terry Perkins is able to stay cool and assume the role of mediator, helping to defuse the situation while Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser flounders helplessly.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor and Nurse Carr carry out tuberculosis tests in the East End slums, while Robert's electro-therapy treatment causes problems. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter _ but when his housekeeper dies suddenly, she changes her opinion. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia expresses her reservations about the artist Lloyd Ellis Parker to James, while Rene is distraught to learn about Sarah's pregnancy. Meanwhile, Carolyn confronts George about Regina, and Elizabeth has lunch with Douglas Goddard.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.sub-title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: George takes Sarah to hospital after finding her at the side of the road, while Olivia continues her relationship with Lloyd, but is distraught when James discovers the affair.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Rose's love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddy's bedroom claims to be his fiance. Starring Patricia Routledge.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toy boy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends the christening of Daisy's granddaughter, and is displeased to learn the child's parents are not married. With some encouragement from Richard, she agrees to keep an open mind, but is later irked when the mother and child go missing. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith.(n)

programme.title: Harrow
programme.desc: The investigator tackles a case in North Queensland involving the discovery on a beach of the body of a parks and conservation officer with a number of shark bites on it. Poisonous jellyfish found in the victim's stomach raise suspicions as to the true nature of his demise. Harrow reconnects with Dr Laurie Badcoe, his other mentor besides Jack, who assures him that Francis Chester is dead. Ioan Gruffudd stars.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: When a troubled gambler is murdered at a greyhound track it seems that his enormous debts have cost him his life. The investigation leads Ross and the team from race-fixing to sex tapes, and in the midst of it all one of their suspects - who include the victim's ex-wife and a dog handler with dark secrets - turns up dead. Detective drama, starring John Michie, Blythe Duff and Alex Norton.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: A woman leaves her estate to her son Alexander, who disappeared in Italy during World War Two on condition he claims the inheritance by 2010. When a mysterious stranger turns up at the funeral to claim the inheritance, he is called an imposter by the rest of the family. The quest for the truth finds Dalziel and Pascoe uncovering dark family secrets, including murder, betrayal, mad dogs, gay bars, young love and a revealing birthmark. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Margaret Tyzack, Jamie Glover and Jack Hedley.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Concluding part. Jonathan Southwood is taken in for questioning, during which a series of shocking revelations point to the true murderer. Walker faces a race against time to reach Connor, who is working undercover at a private club where Colin Thorpe is putting her professionalism to the ultimate test. Crime drama, starring Victoria Smurfit, David Hayman and Nicholas Farrell.(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye.(n)

programme.desc: A murder is committed at the local army barracks using a legendary broken sword, and the incident leads the priest to investigate another death at Dunkirk 13 years previously. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Kembleford's new cricket captain is framed for murder, Father Brown must prove his innocence to give his team any chance of beating neighbouring village Hambleston. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a troubled student at Upcott Seminary leads Father Brown to believe a murderer is at work, and he tries to persuade Sid to go undercover in an attempt to flush out the killer. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When the father of a young writer is found dead, tensions mount against the travellers in Kembleford, and the priest races to find out who actually committed the murder before one of them is wrongly sent to the gallows. Starring Mark Williams, Nathalie Buscombe and Philip McGinley.(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Colour Blind
programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest and his friends find themselves stranded with a group of hedonists and are desperate to escape, fearful that their lives are in danger. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A body is discovered in a vineyard and Father Brown is tasked with searching for the murderer among a group of suspects who are all seeking to reinvent themselves. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest becomes embroiled in an audacious art heist masterminded by his adversary Flambeau, who intends to use Father Brown and his friends to help him settle an old score. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest encounters a man who intends to solve his father's murder a year earlier with the use of his brand-new invention - a time machine. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates when a body is found at the stone circle in a polio-stricken village, leading him to conclude that dark forces are involved - and another sacrifice could be imminent. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a man is found dead inside a locked bank vault, Father Brown must solve the mystery to save an innocent man from paying the ultimate price. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation when he is told during a confession that Bishop Talbot will be assassinated tomorrow. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is stunned when he realises that the fugitive seeking sanctuary at St Mary's is Inspector Sullivan - and the man is wanted for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Tom Chambers and Sorcha Cusack.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and her seasoned colleagues reopen the case of a political aide's murder when a dormant offshore bank account containing £40,000 is discovered in his name. Ben Ransley was killed in 1997 on the evening of Labour's landslide victory and in his wallet was a photo of a beautiful blonde. But neither his mother nor his best friend was able to identify the mystery woman. Is she the key to the crime? Drama, with former Brookside star John McArdle (Billy Corkhill), Jason Durr of Heartbeat fame and one-time Bad Girls actress Kika Mirylees joining Denis Lawson, Dennis Waterman and Nicholas Lyndhurst.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Sam investigates the suspicious death of elderly Jewish brothers Jake and Henry Davies, unearthing evidence of a racist connection. Starring Amanda Burton, Ahsen Bhatti and Andrew Sachs.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII sets out to establish himself as the new sovereign, but finds a dangerous, deeply divided kingdom. Lizzie's loyalties are further complicated by the child she is expecting. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.desc: Transatlantic rivalry breaks out when the officers have to share a barracks with a platoon of American soldiers. Starring Jerome Flynn and Gary Love.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Gina's condition deteriorates, prompting her to ask for Connie's help to ensure a dignified death, but finds her friend is unimpressed with her negative attitude. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Remembrance Day proves eventful in Holby A&E, as Finlay deals with a patient who would prefer to forget the past, and Duffy doesn't need reminding it's her 40th birthday, an occasion complicated by her husband's imminent arrival on the ward. Charlie enjoys a final outing with his Canada-bound son, only for the day to turn into a nightmare. Medical drama, starring Cathy Shipton, Derek Thompson and Kwame Kwei-Armah.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jo Masters and Samantha Nixon continue to hunt for the missing Lesley Palmer - a case given added urgency by the subsequent disappearance of her ex-boyfriend Stewart Jenson. Smithy and Laura Bryant discover the unidentified hospital patient is June's real son, while Terry Perkins and Phil Hunter investigate a sudden death - but soon find they also have a burglary on their hands.(n)

Starring Brian Capron, Keith Barron, Christian Cooke and Kerrie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers a medieval recipe for traditional liquor among some ancient scrolls and goes into full-scale production, building a distillery in his barn and buying up all the potatoes he can find. programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Baby Elsie has been born eight weeks premature, weighs just 860 grams and was born with a hole in her heart. Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Alison organises a sponsored walk to raise money for Joe's class. Volunteering brings the family back together, and also provides Alison with a chance to get to know Heather better. But on the day, Joe refuses to leave his room, the pressure of being the centre of attention proving too much for him. Rebecca comes to his aid and shows him that the walk is not a thing to be feared, and in turn gains the confidence to talk about her feelings concerning her pregnancy and the possibility that her child may also be autistic.(n)

programme.title: Bad Education

Guest starring David Bradley, Ted Robbins and Lethal Bizzle.(n)

programme.desc: As the investigation continues, Carolyn finds out information is being withheld by those close to her, while Eve ) discovers a lead that might bring her closer to Villanelle. programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Basingstoke, Hampshire, to breathe new life into mother-of-five Tania's disorganised garden and turn it into a child-safe and enchanting space.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter K. Highlights include Rick Stein cooking lamb koftas and Raymond Blanc serving up kougelhopf.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is on her home turf in Gloucestershire, where she helps with important conservation work at Slimbridge Wetland Centre, gets a ringside seat to watch some kingfisher parents busy feeding their young and explores the treasures of an ancient bluebell wood. Adam Henson reveals how his horses are helping to create a wildlife haven and Tom Heap investigates how fly-tipping is blighting the countryside more than ever.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With lockdown affecting new filming, Monty Don is at Longmeadow to take an indulgent, horticultural romp through the Gardeners' World archives. From delphiniums to clematis, irises, orchids and dahlias expert growers share their passion along with hints and tips. Plus, Carol Klein visits a garden in Shropshire where she discovers picture perfect plant combinations, Adam Frost travels to the Cotswolds and finds out what happens when plants are brought together with imagination and daring and Nick Bailey offers a guide to the world of Britain's favourite flower - the rose.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith explores the myth and legend surrounding some common garden birds, while Anne Lundon meets the man who fought for his remote village to be connected by road. Arlene Stuart finds out about the incredible lifecycle of the eel and Dougie Vipond learns how rural tourist businesses hope to fight back after lockdown. Plus, a day in the life of a country postie during the pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson hankers after hostas, Kirsty Wilson harks back to the '70s to create an easy terrarium and Chris Beardshaw deals with a very bad bout of Box Blight.(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prove that home-made can beat shop-bought when they prepare gravlax, jam doughnuts and their own corned beef.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: The Unnatural History of London: Natural World
programme.desc: Exploring the London's surprising and secret wildlife, including seals, parakeets, badgers, foxes and even pelicans who have made London their home. The programme also focuses on the admirers of these animals, from Billingsgate fish porters and Indian chefs to Crayfish Bob, who scours the canals for foreign species.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by the Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin investigates the history of highwaymen in Surrey in an episode from the Watts Gallery, where visitors arrive with their antiques and collectables to get them valued.(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers.(n)

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary exploring the country in the aftermath of the recent humanitarian crisis in which thousands of Rohingya Muslims were driven from their homes by the military. Simon visits Burma's Buddhist heartlands, including the ancient capital of Bagan, and meets the city's controversial nationalist monks. While the region affected by the military crackdown is off limits, he is able to visit the world's biggest refugee camp in Bangladesh, and meet people traumatised by the violence.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from the past 17 series of the business ideas contest, featuring Dragons past and present getting to grips with ingenious inventions and bizarre money-making schemes. The show recalls some of the harshest dismissals, but also the times when entrepreneurs got the chance to make their dream a reality.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The rapper prepares to open a show for big-name star Meek Mill. He asks GaTa to be his hype man on stage. However, as GaTa's behaviour becomes more and more erratic, it may be impossible to keep his biggest secret from his new friends.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The rapper and his crew travel to his home town of Philadelphia, where he is about to give his first big live show as opening act for Meek Mill.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: A murderous raid backfires for EZ and the gang when a covert ally is caught, while Angel's animosity to his brother troubles their chief.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ and Angel embark on a rift-healing road trip. Back home an even older relationship is revisited, and Emily loses her cool.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A yoga teacher and her partner have their hearts set on a water birth, but when they arrive at the hospital, they are confronted with an unforeseen obstacle to their plans. A 22-year-old woman whose pregnancy was unplanned needs to be induced, but the birth progresses very slowly. However, her mother and her boyfriend are both on hand to support her through the struggle. A woman who had part of her cervix removed while suffering from cancer prepares to give birth to her third child, and reveals how her new partner has become a vital presence in the lives of her two daughters.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor celebrates the heroic efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers during the coronavirus crisis bidding to make a difference by helping the vulnerable and NHS.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers, Rachel de Thame, Toby Buckland and Frances Tophill reveal some of the most beautiful flowers that are really easy to grow and those plants that need virtually no attention. Plus, ideas for low maintenance lawns, border planting that takes very little effort and why spending very little time on orchids could make them even prettier. Presented by Nicki Chapman.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: With more than half of the world's primates under threat of extinction, Chris Packham narrates a look at what is being done to protect primates, with scientists making ground-breaking discoveries about this remarkable animal family. Researcher Dr Cat Hobaiter has spent 13 years studying chimpanzees in the jungles of Uganda, while on Thailand's East coast, Dr Amanda Tan reveals how long-tailed macaques use tools to harvest shellfish.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: The last in a set of four documentaries exploring wildlife and plants throughout the year focuses on the spring, when hibernating animals awake and many species search for mates. The programme features footage of grizzly bear cubs, the mating dances of grebes, flowers growing in the desert and swifts embarking on marathon journeys.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don helps Oxford neighbours Anne and Fliss, who want to combine the front gardens of their semi-detached homes into one single space filled with their favourite grasses and perennials. The gardening enthusiasts have some big ideas - but can they work together to achieve one goal? He also travels to Pewsey, where former Army officer Sally is converting a tarmac and rubble-covered area into a cosy, wildlife-friendly haven for her daughters. She wants it ready for summer, which doesn't give them a great deal of time - but she does have an army of friends willing to get involved.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.sub-title: Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by legendary Hollywood double act Ginger Rogers and Fred Astaire, capturing the milestones and highlights of their lives and careers. Narrated by veteran actress Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Top Hat (1935)
programme.desc: A woman on holiday in London meets a famous dancer staying in the same hotel when his enthusiastic rehearsing wakes her up. Attraction instantly develops between them, but she mistakenly believes he is a producer married to her friend. Musical, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers, Helen Broderick and Edward Everett Horton, with a score by Irving Berlin, including songs Cheek to Cheek and White Tie and Tails.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From West Somerset at Crowcombe Court, an English mansion that dates back to 1739. Antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot finds a tribal weapon that was part of a collection gathered by a sea captain on his travels and Thomas Plant hears all about a Victorian gin bottle that was buried in a tip excavated to make way for the London Olympic ground. Presenter Paul Martin follows in the footsteps of Romantic poet Samuel Taylor Coleridge, who wrote his best-known work whilst he was living in Somerset.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble celebrate Longleat's families, looking back at archive footage of the safari park's first visitors and the moment 50 lions were released. They also follow keepers as the Animal Park team attempts to film the birth of some eagerly awaited cheetah cubs.(n)

programme.title: Code-Breakers: Bletchley Park's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: Keeley Hawes narrates the story of how mathematician Bill Tutte and GPO engineer Tommy Flowers unravelled the secrets of Adolf Hitler's personal code machine - reputedly even more secure than the Enigma device. The duo's work played an important role in ending the conflict in Europe, but because of its classified nature and continued application during the Cold War, the two men were never officially recognised.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace celebrates some of the behind-the-scenes heroes who are working around the clock in the nation's factories to ensure that cupboards at home are kept stocked. In the first edition, Gregg goes to the Essity paper mill in Manchester, which he visited back in 2018 and where they have upped production to make a million rolls of toilet paper every day, seven days a week.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull continues their travels around Britain, this time seeking out the birthplaces of the inventions that fuelled the nation's industrial infrastructure. Their first stop is the Royal Institution in London, where they examine how the Davy lamp saved the lives of thousands of coal miners, then pay a visit to the preserved laboratory of Michael Faraday, another of their scientific heroes, whose research on electricity paved the way for its application as a ubiquitous energy supply.(n)

programme.title: Suburbicon (2017)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1959, a mild-mannered family man must face his demons after a home invasion shakes his quaint neighbourhood, as he has to navigate the town's dark underbelly of betrayal, deceit, and violence. Black comedy directed by George Clooney, starring Matt Damon, Julianne Moore and Oscar Isaac.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: A Horizon Special - Part Two
programme.desc: Dr Chris van Tulleken, Dr Hannah Fry and Michael Mosley examine the latest research and explore some of the big questions about Covid-19 and the pandemic it has created. Michael visits the UK government's high-security laboratory Porton Down, where vaccines are being tested. He also uncovers what the experience of the 2002 SARs epidemic reveals about this one. Both were caused by coronaviruses, but certain key changes to today's virus have allowed it to infect the world, where SARs was contained.(n)

programme.title: 54 Hours: The Gladbeck Hostage Crisis
programme.desc: Part one of two. Two robbers hold up a bank and demand free passage from the police while taking members of staff hostage. However, the attempt to free the hostages results in a series of disastrous mistakes by the police and the media. Fact-based crime drama about a crisis that shocked Germany in the summer of 1988, starring Sascha Alexander Gersak and Alexander Scheer.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is on her home turf in Gloucestershire, where she helps with important conservation work at Slimbridge Wetland Centre, gets a ringside seat to watch some kingfisher parents busy feeding their young and explores the treasures of an ancient bluebell wood. Adam Henson reveals how his horses are helping to create a wildlife haven and Tom Heap investigates how fly-tipping is blighting the countryside more than ever.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces more footage of near misses, featuring the rescue of a hiker who was searching for his dog when a cliff crumbled beneath his feet.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay shares creative and money-saving tips and tricks for the home and garden, including how to build a floating desk and upholster an old stool. Plus, Sarah Moore demonstrates a way to recycle old jars, and Monty Don shares a fantastic method of making a raised bed for the garden.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The team takes on a pocket-sized racing car, a century-old weather vane, and a much loved but horribly damaged leather rhinoceros.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: With the sun shining and the flowers blooming, summer is a season of splendid abundance, and the long hours of daylight make life burst out in a riot of activity. However, in certain regions there can be sweltering heat. As this documentary reveals, for a range of species, from sneaky ringtailed lemurs to battling ibex and penguins to colour-changing lizards, summer is a time when the living is not always easy. Narrated by Andrew Scott.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Worthing couple Emma and Josh want to transform their overgrown plot into a family garden for their son Noah, who has Down's syndrome. Monty Don helps them learn about different sensory plants - only for rain to jeopardise the project. In Hitchin, Aletheia is determined to create a neighbourhood vegetable patch and a trip to Cranbrook Community Garden in London proves it is possible - but can she persuade the other locals to join in?(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the TV appearances made over the years by Hollywood star Robert Mitchum, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career.(n)

programme.title: The Magnificent Ambersons (1942)
programme.desc: A successful car manufacturer is reunited with his lost love, now a wealthy high society widow, and hopes to give their relationship a second chance. Her selfish son strives to keep them apart, as industrial progress leaves the whole family facing an uncertain future. Period drama, written, directed and narrated by Orson Welles, starring Joseph Cotten and Agnes Moorehead.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bletchley Park in Buckinghamshire, where antique experts Charlie Ross and Mark Stacey find an incredible autograph book and a beautiful tea set.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The keepers battle to save the life of a newborn reindeer calf that is too weak to suckle from its mother. A nest-cam is used to monitor a pair of rainbow lorikeet eggs.(n)

programme.title: Little Ships: The Miracle of Dunkirk
programme.desc: Dan Snow tells the story of the boats that played their part in lifting thousands of Allied troops off the beaches of Dunkirk in the week straddling late May and early June, 1940. Meeting some of the owners, he finds out what happened to the 700-strong flotilla of trawlers, pleasure craft and lifeboats and follows preparations to mark the 70th anniversary of the event as 50 of the surviving vessels return to France.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: A new format of the annual wildlife series in keeping with Covid-19 lockdown conditions, which sees the presenters broadcasting live from locations around the UK. Chris Packham is in the New Forest, Iolo William is in the heart of west Wales, and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver project near her home in Cornwall. In this first episode, they are joined by guest presenter Steve Backshall at his home on the banks of the Thames. Plus, Michaela Strachan calls live from South Africa to present some of her favourite moments from past series.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the history of an 18th-century sea captain's house near Bristol's docks with links to the slave trade. Bristol was a major port in the transportation of slaves from Africa's Guinea Coast to the Caribbean, and both the man who built the house and its first full-time resident were heavily involved in the trade. David uncovers stories of piracy, abandoned babies, a household slave who made a daring escape from the property, and a protest on the doorstep as the abolitionist movement gained momentum.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Dotty, Russell Kane, Richard Madeley, Steph McGovern and Steve Pemberton go for dinner together in this extended episode. They must place their bank card in the centre of the restaurant table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk: The Soldiers' Story
programme.desc: Survivors of the British Expeditionary Force recall their reaction to the German army's blitzkrieg on May 10, 1940, which saw Holland and Belgium overrun. Few of these soldiers had experienced modern mechanised warfare or even been abroad before, so the environment of death, dive-bombing and fear came as a shattering blow, both physically and mentally.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Five percussion soloists compete for a place in the semi-finals, performing works by John Psathas, Sergei Golovko, Georges Aperghis, Astor Piazzolla and Jago Thornton. Anna Lapwood is joined by former percussion winner Owen Gunnell to review the performances, recorded in the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Cairngorms: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess.(n)

programme.title: Revisor by Crystal Pite and Jonathon Young
programme.desc: A performance of the critically acclaimed dance-theatre production, created by award-winning choreographer Crystal Pite and playwright Jonathon Young, recorded during its run at Sadler's Wells in March. Revisor is based on the well-known play The Government Inspector by Nikolai Gogol, and Young and Pite revised the archetypal comic plot to serve as the basis for a production that blends contemporary theatre and dance.(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.sub-title: Russia - A Century of Suspicion: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Military historian Saul David draws on Timewatch documentaries and a wide range of BBC archive to examine how television has portrayed Russia through the years.(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how New Zealand's indigenous Maori people resisted colonisation and marginalisation, and maintained their distinctive culture, so much so that it is now an integral part of modern New Zealand and celebrated all over the world. He shows how, from the beginning of their encounters with Europeans, the Maori adapted outside influences, whether it was modern firearms or the new religion of Christianity, and produced fascinating hybrid work that ranged from elaborately carved rifle butts to a Madonna and Child statue adorned with the Ta Moko, the sacred Maori facial tattoo.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Five percussion soloists compete for a place in the semi-finals, performing works by John Psathas, Sergei Golovko, Georges Aperghis, Astor Piazzolla and Jago Thornton. Anna Lapwood is joined by former percussion winner Owen Gunnell to review the performances, recorded in the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Cairngorms: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene in which he shows how the exciting colours of autumn can be captured on canvas.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: The return of the documentary offering an insight into the world of modern and classical dance. The first edition follows Strictly Come Dancing professionals Gorka Marquez and Karen Hauer as they rehearse choreography for a new Latin dance spectacular.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines how the 1960s saw Harold Wilson become leader of a rapidly changing country excited by new technology, modern architecture and a spirit of progress. He then discovers why the liberated dream of that decade turned into the industrial malaise and generational conflicts of Edward Heath's Britain in the 1970s.(n)

programme.title: The Cult of Sunday Night
programme.desc: Examining the popularity of family drama series All Creatures Great and Small, which followed the adventures of rural Yorkshire vet James Herriot (played by Christopher Timothy), and whose mix of nostalgia, romance, eccentric characters and stunning scenery could be said to have set the template for several Sunday night dramas that would be aired in years to come. However, the cast and crew reveal things were often much less cosy behind the scenes, thanks to accidents, cantankerous actors and the limited supply of source material. Narrated by Meera Syal.(n)

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James Herriot visits Yorkshire seeking work as a vet with Siegfried Farnon's practice, but his interview becomes a baptism of fire when goes out on the rounds in the Dales countryside. The first episode of the drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy.(n)

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James discovers the downside of life as a vet when the post-mortem results for Lord Hulton's horse arrive, while Siegfried's younger brother Tristan comes back from college and James is introduced to Mrs Pumphrey and her dog Tricki Woo.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Hurricanes and Heatwaves - The Highs and Lows of British Weather
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the evolution of the weather forecast, from print via radio to TV and beyond, featuring an insight into how the Met Office and the BBC have always used the latest technology to bring the holy grail of accurate forecasting that much closer. Yet, as hand-drawn maps have been replaced by weather apps, the bigger drama of global warming has been playing itself out, as if to prove that people were right all along to obsess about the weather.(n)

programme.title: Everything Is Connected - George Eliot's Life
programme.desc: In this Arena programme, contemporary artist Gillian Wearing celebrates the legacy of Victorian novelist George Eliot. Just as Eliot's novel Middlemarch explored the lives of ordinary men and women, this experimental film is made up of a diverse cast of people from different backgrounds, and features Jason Isaacs and Sheila Atim as the narrators.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene in which he shows how the exciting colours of autumn can be captured on canvas.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: The return of the documentary offering an insight into the world of modern and classical dance. The first edition follows Strictly Come Dancing professionals Gorka Marquez and Karen Hauer as they rehearse choreography for a new Latin dance spectacular.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines how the 1960s saw Harold Wilson become leader of a rapidly changing country excited by new technology, modern architecture and a spirit of progress. He then discovers why the liberated dream of that decade turned into the industrial malaise and generational conflicts of Edward Heath's Britain in the 1970s.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Sharon Eyal - A Basic Instinct
programme.desc: Following Sharon Eyal and Gai Behar create, rehearse and premiere a new dance, performed on the top of a multistorey car park in Peckham.(n)

programme.title: Chemistry - A Volatile History
programme.desc: Professor of Theoretical Physics Jim Al-Khalili charts the development of the Periodic Table and follows in the footsteps of the pioneers who discovered the elements that form the building blocks of the world.(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: A compendium of three short plays written and performed in isolation - Viral by James Graham, aboput three teenagers in pursuit of the next internet craze, Penny by Charlene James, starring Lennie James as a man living in a hotel wrestling with his own survival, and Going Forward by John Donnelly, in which a team leader takes drastic measures to ensure targets are met.(n)

programme.title: A Year in an English Garden: Flicker & Pulse
programme.desc: Brian McClave and Tom Wichelow's striking and poignant portrayal of time passing in a beautiful walled garden in the south-east of England. Using real-time and time-lapse footage, the film explores the relationship between the seasons and the plants and people who work within the walls of the garden. Locked into the clock of the solar system, the garden performs its annual display, guided by those passionately engaged with its soil.(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook examines sci-fi's obsession with alien invasion, from all-out assault to sinister hidden threats. He explores how it has reflected real-life anxieties - whether they be the challenge to Victorian imperial power in HG Wells's The War of the Worlds, the Cold War paranoia in Invasion of the Body Snatchers or more recent concerns about racism and immigration in 2009 movie District 9. He also celebrates one of the most famous alien invaders of all time - the Dalek. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who showrunner Steven Moffat and former star David Tennant, actor Richard Dreyfuss (Close Encounters of the Third Kind), Independence Day director Roland Emmerich and special effects supervisors Phil Tippett (Star Wars, Jurassic Park, RoboCop) and Douglas Trumbull (2001: A Space Odyssey, Close Encounters, Blade Runner).(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon tries to distract herself but gets more than she bargained for when she drops off some leftover baby items at Karen's and finds herself watching baby Kayden. Phil puts pressure on Ben to not mess up the job, unaware his son has done his own secret deal, while Jean dresses up in the hope of impressing Phil and persuading him to let Stacey come home. Keegan's dreams come crashing down when he admits to Iqra that he did not sort out insurance on the vandalised food truck.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Baby Elsie has been born eight weeks premature, weighs just 860 grams and was born with a hole in her heart. Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Alison organises a sponsored walk to raise money for Joe's class. Volunteering brings the family back together, and also provides Alison with a chance to get to know Heather better. But on the day, Joe refuses to leave his room, the pressure of being the centre of attention proving too much for him. Rebecca comes to his aid and shows him that the walk is not a thing to be feared, and in turn gains the confidence to talk about her feelings concerning her pregnancy and the possibility that her child may also be autistic.(n)

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with the voices of Ben Whishaw and Imelda Staunton.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns unannounced, keen to try again with Phil - although she finds it hard being back in the Square. Keegan is touched when Tiffany presents him with a special gift, only for his frustrations to continue after an altercation at the food truck. Mo makes a worrying discovery about Jean, while the Carters raise cash by selling off their old items - upsetting Tina when they mistake some of her possessions for junk.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Marianne is on a student exchange programme in Sweden for a year and while her break-up with Jamie cost her some of her friends, she is still in touch with Connell and Joanna. Word of her sex life with photographer Lukas makes its way back to Dublin, and when Connell bumps into Peggy at a party, she asks him about the gossip and whether things were like that with him and Marianne. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones, with India Mullen and Lancelot Ncube.(n)

Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Silicon Glen: From Ships to Microchips
programme.desc: The story of IBM's facility in Spango Valley, Greenock, which provided the region with thousands of jobs at the cutting edge of modern technology from the post-war period to its closure in 2018, as well as drawing investments to Scotland from all over the world. The programme features the stories of former employees, and discovers the role the workforce played in the moon landing and the making of classic film 2001: A Space Odyssey.(n)

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with the voices of Ben Whishaw and Imelda Staunton.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns unannounced, keen to try again with Phil - although she finds it hard being back in the Square. Keegan is touched when Tiffany presents him with a special gift, only for his frustrations to continue after an altercation at the food truck. Mo makes a worrying discovery about Jean, while the Carters raise cash by selling off their old items - upsetting Tina when they mistake some of her possessions for junk.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Marianne is on a student exchange programme in Sweden for a year and while her break-up with Jamie cost her some of her friends, she is still in touch with Connell and Joanna. Word of her sex life with photographer Lukas makes its way back to Dublin, and when Connell bumps into Peggy at a party, she asks him about the gossip and whether things were like that with him and Marianne. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones, with India Mullen and Lancelot Ncube.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell struggles with severe depression after a tragic event and his mental health deteriorates further when he returns to college, struggling to drag himself to lectures. At Niall's suggestion, he begins counselling, while Marianne attempts to reassure him over Skype as she continues her studies in Sweden. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: It is parents' evening at Abbey Grove School, and unconventional teacher Alfie is under pressure to mark his mock exam papers. However, after discovering that biology teacher Miss Gulliver has split from her boyfriend, he becomes distracted from his work and determined to secure a date with her. First episode of the comedy written by and starring Jack Whitehall, with Mathew Horne and Sarah Solemani.(n)

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: The parents of pupils at Abbey Grove School are worried about the impending arrival of French exchange students after they ran riot the previous year, leaving a host of love-struck teenagers in their wake. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon tries to distract herself but gets more than she bargained for when she drops off some leftover baby items at Karen's and finds herself watching baby Kayden. Phil puts pressure on Ben to not mess up the job, unaware his son has done his own secret deal, while Jean dresses up in the hope of impressing Phil and persuading him to let Stacey come home. Keegan's dreams come crashing down when he admits to Iqra that he did not sort out insurance on the vandalised food truck.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Baby Elsie has been born eight weeks premature, weighs just 860 grams and was born with a hole in her heart. Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Alison organises a sponsored walk to raise money for Joe's class. Volunteering brings the family back together, and also provides Alison with a chance to get to know Heather better. But on the day, Joe refuses to leave his room, the pressure of being the centre of attention proving too much for him. Rebecca comes to his aid and shows him that the walk is not a thing to be feared, and in turn gains the confidence to talk about her feelings concerning her pregnancy and the possibility that her child may also be autistic.(n)

In the seaside city of Aqaba he uses local molasses in a fattoush salad, and cooks with the chefs at a swanky golf club, where roasted fish with spicy rice is the order of the day. Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. Back in his kitchen, chicken shawarma with tahini sauce and pitta bread packed with spiced beef mince are the final dishes on the menu.(n)

Alan Titchmarsh's challenge is to create a more child-friendly garden for their terraced home in Tonbridge, and his first task is to make a shady environment so the kids can spend time outside in the day. After that, he and the team come up with a bespoke lighting system for the evenings, acoustic fencing to cut down the noise from a nearby railway line, and an area for the parents.(n)

programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Gareth Ward for a tour of Mid Wales. They begin at a cider-tasting, which James uses to dish up some cider-infused apple fritters. Gareth discovers a farm where the Wagyu cattle fed on local beer. Away from the culinary activities, Gareth is challenged to a rally car ride and James demonstrates his dog training prowess with his trusty hound Cooper.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is still in the eastern Mediterranean country of Jordan. In the seaside city of Aqaba he uses local molasses in a fattoush salad, and cooks with the chefs at a swanky golf club, where roasted fish with spicy rice is the order of the day. Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. Back in his kitchen, chicken shawarma with tahini sauce and pitta bread packed with spiced beef mince are the final dishes on the menu.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Kent couple Lou and Andy Webb have two young children affected by a rare genetic condition that severely restricts their eyesight, meaning they only see in black and white and have to wear special goggles in sunlight. Alan Titchmarsh's challenge is to create a more child-friendly garden for their terraced home in Tonbridge, and his first task is to make a shady environment so the kids can spend time outside in the day. After that, he and the team come up with a bespoke lighting system for the evenings, acoustic fencing to cut down the noise from a nearby railway line, and an area for the parents.(n)

In the fourth episode, the three brothers track down another branch of their growing family tree, before being put through their paces in a brutal yoga session. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. Barnaby and Troy discover Hadleigh was a mystery man, with no relatives or marriage certificate to match the wedding photo on display in his house. To make matters worse, another body is then found. Mystery, guest starring Anna Massey and Una Stubbs.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the storm, Rowan takes charge of the situation as the residents try to free Charlotte from beneath the rubble and rescue Danny, who has been left behind by Alice in her desperate search for help. Vanessa and Rowan use a helicopter to eventually spot him lying unconscious in the Bush and take him home. The next morning, Alice decides to pack her bags and leave, but a kiss from Danny might prompt a change of heart.(n)

programme.desc: Ned is proud of Jan for attacking Kim. Zak frightens Eric into giving him a share of the profits from the fight. Zoe tells Frank about the church blessing.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mildred becomes broody and starts to dream of motherhood, much to George's dismay - and they consider adoption. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.sub-title: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mike faces a stressful time as he struggles to cope with his wife's departure, and Tricia offers him a shoulder to cry on, but is beset by problems of her own after prescribing birth control to a 16-year-old. Meanwhile, Sergeant Craddock finds his efforts to prepare for an important visit hampered by a series of attacks on the station, and Vernon makes a startling discovery in his bathtub. Jason Durr and Clare Calbraith star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe wants Frank to give his blessing to the wedding. Kim is not happy with the new housekeeper.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is furious with Frank, while Kim and Dave receive a frosty welcome in the Woolpack. Butch tries to exert some authority over the rest of the Dingles.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Gail he does not want to fight Don. The football team wins its first match and Curly is permanently dropped. Derek snaps and tells Norris he has had enough of him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Platts try to explain to Sarah that Nick changing his name will not stop him being her brother. Raquel feels she is being messed around by Leo when he postpones their date.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mike faces a stressful time as he struggles to cope with his wife's departure, and Tricia offers him a shoulder to cry on, but is beset by problems of her own after prescribing birth control to a 16-year-old. Meanwhile, Sergeant Craddock finds his efforts to prepare for an important visit hampered by a series of attacks on the station, and Vernon makes a startling discovery in his bathtub. Jason Durr and Clare Calbraith star.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Sybil's determination to pursue her political leanings lands her in great danger when a fight breaks out at an election results meeting. Matthew's tenderness in caring for the would-be suffragette is noticed by her sisters, but it is Mary who is stunned to receive a marriage proposal. Bates is cleared of suspicion over the stolen wine, but offers his resignation rather than name the culprit. Drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Important comedy milestones of 1998, including The Royle Family and Goodness Gracious Me, and the final episodes of Drop the Dead Donkey and Father Ted. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)
