programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Cora makes a discovery that throws the inheritance issue into chaos, and Mary is prompted to keep Matthew waiting for an answer to his proposal. Robert arranges for Mrs Patmore to see an eye specialist, and O'Brien digs up more compromising information about Bates, but the Earl's response comes as a surprise. Thomas leaves the Abbey and Mary exacts vengeance on Edith for her treachery, while the household is rocked by a shocking announcement. Drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo).(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. Barnaby and Troy discover Hadleigh was a mystery man, with no relatives or marriage certificate to match the wedding photo on display in his house. To make matters worse, another body is then found. Mystery, guest starring Anna Massey and Una Stubbs.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the storm, Rowan takes charge of the situation as the residents try to free Charlotte from beneath the rubble and rescue Danny, who has been left behind by Alice in her desperate search for help. Vanessa and Rowan use a helicopter to eventually spot him lying unconscious in the Bush and take him home. The next morning, Alice decides to pack her bags and leave, but a kiss from Danny might prompt a change of heart.(n)

programme.desc: Charity seeks out Priya for help, Harriet and Malone play with fire, and Moira and Cain talk about the divorce.(n)

programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. Drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo).(n)

programme.desc: An endless cacophony of loud noises from next door drives Mr Bean to distraction, filling his mind with thoughts of revenge.(n)

programme.desc: Faced with a long shopping list from his formidable landlady, the hapless half-wit builds a giant motorised trolley from a petrol mower and pram wheels, hoping to get the job done in double-quick time.(n)

programme.desc: Former track and field athlete Jessica Ennis-Hill, the golden girl of the 2012 Olympics, dons an apron to cook for diners at Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty's cafe at the end of Southend Pier. On the menu is a seafood feast of pan-seared cod wrapped in Iberico ham, with smoked almonds, clams, fennel marmalade, olives and baby courgette. Jamie whips up a delicious katsu curry, while Jimmy's DIY skills come in handy as he creates a chocolate fountain for his brother's wedding. The hosts also look to crack the problem of using eggs from caged hens once and for all.(n)

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer present the food and chat show live from the studio. Comedian Tom Allen chats about the new series of Bake Off: The Professionals, actress Eve Myles talks about the TV thriller We Hunt Together, Charlatans frontman Tim Burgess discusses his online listening parties and actor Russell Tovey talks about his podcast, Talk Art, and his radio play, The Understudy. Plus there's music by Tom Grennan and Badly Drawn Boy.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge attend a confectionery convention, where they plan to make off with lots of free sweets. Unfortunately, Homer's love of candy results in him being wrongly accused of sexual harassment by the family's babysitter and smeared by the media. Featuring the guest voice of Dennis Franz (NYPD Blue).(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Apu has the bright idea of turning the Kwik-E-Mart into a novel money-spinning tourist attraction, while Lisa is struck by the awful thought that her genetic make-up means she will follow in her family's footsteps and end up stupid.(n)

programme.title: Megamind (2010)
programme.desc: An evil genius finally succeeds in killing the superhero who has foiled his every evil scheme, but finds life without an enemy boring. To fill the gap, he transforms an ordinary man into a worthy adversary - but when his creation turns evil, it falls to the villain to save the world. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Ferrell, Tina Fey and Brad Pitt.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired pair Retired Dawn and Les from Basingstoke want to relocate to sunny south-east Cyprus, where they're looking for a townhouse or bungalow that all four generations of their family can enjoy. The couple used to be leaders in the Scouts and the Brownies, so Jonnie Irwin has to work hard to earn his house hunting badge, as he comes up with five properties for their £100,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kerr Drummond and Kirsty Duffy help a couple look for their first home together in Kent, despite both wanting an area similar to the different places where they grew up.(n)

programme.title: Penelope Keith's Hidden Villages
programme.desc: The actress is in Devon and Cornwall as she explores more of the nation's smallest communities. It's a land of rugged coastal communities and distinct identities forged over centuries, but also a region visited by five million people each year. Penelope travels to a former silver-mining village, a cliff-edge fishing village, the most exposed theatre in the country, and the small communities near Fowey Harbour.(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas

programme.title: How to Go on Holiday This Summer
programme.desc: Steph McGovern investigates whether it is possible to get away safely this summer, answering some of the burning questions, whether travelling abroad or holidaying in the UK. From whether we should we book now and what the least risky way to travel is, to the trips we've already paid for and if we should cancel them and seek a refund or cross our fingers.(n)

programme.title: The Fantastical Factory of Curious Craft
programme.desc: Keith Lemon challenges stay-at-home dad Simon, aeronautical engineer Adam, art student Kisa and designer and part-time clown Gem to create landmarks from around the world that must illuminate, in a bid to impress Keith and be picked to go through to create a one-off craft for this week's celebrity client, Spice Girl Mel B. Keith chooses his favourite three to go on to craft for Mel, who wants a bespoke feature for her well-being room. Whose creation will Mel really, really want and choose to take home?(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Levison Wood reaches the final stage of his epic 650-mile journey, following the annual migration of Botswana's 130,000 elephants. Together with his guide Kane, Lev follows the elephants deep into the heart of the Okavango Delta, a vast wetland teeming with wildlife where every year more than 40,000 elephants gather to feed. Lev and Kane narrowly avoid a pride of lions who have brought down a male buffalo and watch as they move in for the kill, but are forced to wade through wide channels where crocodiles, hippos and snakes lurk.(n)

Elsewhere in Worcestershire, the enforcement agents try to recover more than £10,000 from a shop-keeper for fraudulently keeping a deposit during a property sale.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Finally, Stewart and Vic head to Lancashire to recover over £3,000 owed by a garage to a dissatisfied customer.(n)

programme.title: Godzilla (1998)
programme.desc: A giant mutant lizard spawned from an atomic test rampages through the streets of New York, defying the combined might of the US Army and Navy as it searches for a quiet place to hatch its eggs. A bookish radiation expert and a shady French secret agent join forces to defeat the creature. Hollywood remake of the Japanese monster adventure, starring Matthew Broderick, Jean Reno, Maria Pitillo and Hank Azaria.(n)

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle Enigma: Revealed
programme.desc: Documentary about the area of the north Atlantic between Florida, Puerto Rico and Bermuda where it is alleged many boats and aeroplanes have gone missing. Stories about strange events in the region include a US Coast Guard ship that detected a phantom landmass on its radar, two 'ghost rockets' that flew silently over a US Navy ship and Russian submarines that reported all manner of bizarre activity. There are interviews with some of the leading experts on this mystery, air traffic controllers Bob Greene and Bob Gish, pilots Bruce Gernon and Fred Grieser, underwater explorers Evan Kovacs and Les Savege and weather expert Dr Stephen Leatherman.(n)

programme.title: Filthy House SOS

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Single mum Claire Lee swaps her three-bedroom Wolverhampton terraced house to spend a week in the nearby five-bedroom home of cardiologist and paediatrician Mahrous and Rana Alsoud - and vice versa - to see how the other half lives. Claire and her five children will see their weekly budget increase from £171 to a massive £3,000, while Rana is hoping the swap will give her children a taste of her own upbringing in Syria.(n)

programme.title: Most Shocking Celebrity Moments
programme.sub-title: Most Shocking TV Moments of the 90s
programme.desc: A countdown of infamous incidents from the 1990s, including actor Hugh Grant's arrest in Los Angeles and OJ Simpson's murder trial. The programme also looks back at Manchester United star Eric Cantona kicking a fan during a match against Crystal Palace, Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker interrupting Michael Jackson's performance at the Brit awards, and footballer David Beckham's lavish wedding to Victoria Adams of the Spice Girls. Featuring contributions from Louis Walsh, Shaun Ryder, Bez, Michael Burke, Darren Day, Gail Porter, Tim Vincent, Liz McClarnon and Kerry Katona.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A 500-mile journey across South Africa in a luxury hotel train, which travels between Pretoria and Durban on the coast over the course of three days. The first stop along the way is Nambiti Private Game Reserve, home to rhinos and elephants, before moving on to the country's most famous battlefields and the Drakensberg Mountains. Passengers have a chance to explore the ancient art of the San people and visit a vineyard that produces some of South Africa's finest wines. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue a fleeing Vauxhall Astra through the streets of north Hartlepool at more than 100mph, but are forced to call the chase off and the driver later robs a supermarket at knife-point, sparking a huge manhunt. Liam and his sniffer dog Louie take part in the search of a house that uncovers thousands in cash and nearly £500,000 in class A drugs, while firearms officers rush to Guisborough at 11pm on a Friday after a caller claims that a man with a shotgun is walking around the town.(n)

programme.title: Police: Hour of Duty

programme.desc: A Lilliputian lope round the Inner Hebrides puts Jane in touch with her Scottish roots, as alongside just six other passengers the singer embarks on what is easily her smallest ever cruise. Jane enjoys unseasonably cloudless skies and sparkling seas as the route takes in the puffin-packed Treshnish Islands and Fingal's Cave on Staffa, the mythic rock formation that famously inspired Mendelssohn's Hebrides Overture. The excursion is rounded off with some traditional trappings, from ceilidhs and weaving to haggis-making.(n)

programme.desc: The actor goes on rail journeys around the globe, jumping on and off trains to find adventure and face challenges, while exploring the history and culture of each place he visits.(n)

programme.title: Dogs with Incredible Jobs
programme.desc: Featuring a rescue schnauzer cross that was trained to drive a car, and detector dogs that patrol New Zealand's islands for rats or mice that pose a threat to endangered species. In a minimum security prison, dogs from a rescue shelter play a vital role in helping prisoners preparing to transition back into society to work on their socialisation skills, while volunteers and their dogs pay visits to people with dementia and palliative care centres.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.sub-title: The Yorkshire Vet: The Peter Wright Story
programme.desc: Peter looks back on the highs and lows of his veterinary career. He returns to his childhood home for the first time in 50 years and visiting his former teachers from primary school to sixth form, who reveal the events that inspired his medical career. He also looks back on his experiences as a work experience student for Alf Wight, also known to the world as James Herriot.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: Return to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild Revisited
programme.desc: Ben Fogle reunites with people he previously met in some of the most remote locations on Earth, discovering how life has changed for his hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Murder of Rhys Jones: What Happened Next
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of 11-year-old Rhys Jones in Liverpool in August 2007, an innocent child caught in the crossfire of a city's gang culture. The programme tells the tragic tale through interviews with friends, police on the case, and key politicians who had to deal with the fallout from a murder that shocked the nation.(n)

programme.title: The EastEnders Murders: True Crime, Real Tragedy
programme.desc: A documentary exploring the stories behind the deaths of Gemma McCluskie and Sian Blake, who were both stars of one of Britain's best-loved soaps, EastEnders. Gemma McCluskie was beaten to death in 2012 by her own brother, while Sian Blake and her two young sons were reported missing in 2015, before her partner pleaded guilty to the murders.(n)

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1979)
programme.desc: An American heiress befriends an elderly lady on a continental train journey, but wakes one morning to find she has disappeared and the other passengers deny having ever seen the missing woman - leading to suspicions that a conspiracy is afoot. Remake of Hitchcock's 1938 mystery, starring Elliott Gould, Cybill Shepherd, Angela Lansbury, Herbert Lom, Arthur Lowe and Ian Carmichael.(n)

programme.title: The Three Stooges (2012)
programme.desc: Three babies left abandoned on an orphanage doorstep grow up into a bungling, accident prone trio of maintenance men. When the children's home is threatened with closure, they try to raise the money to save it, getting drawn into a murder plot and becoming the stars of a reality TV show. Slapstick comedy, with Sean Hayes, Will Sasso and Chris Diamantopoulos.(n)

programme.title: The Last Airbender (2010)
programme.desc: A chosen one with mastery of the four elements is charged with keeping the peace between four nations. When he disappears while still a boy, one of these factions sees a chance to gain absolute power and wages war on the others. After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age: Collision Course (2016)
programme.desc: When a cosmic blunder leaves Earth in the path of an asteroid, it's up to Sid the sloth and pals Manny and Diego to save the day. Animated sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, Denis Leary, John Leguizamo and Simon Pegg.(n)

programme.title: The Madness of King George (1994)
programme.desc: British monarch George III's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic, prompting fears that he is going insane. While physicians try to uncover the cause of his condition, the king's enemies in Parliament and the frustrated Prince of Wales conspire to have him declared insane and removed from the throne. Period drama based on Alan Bennett's play, starring Nigel Hawthorne, Helen Mirren and Rupert Everett.(n)

programme.title: Snowpiercer (2013)
programme.desc: In a snow-ravaged world, a group of travellers fight for survival on a train that perpetually circumnavigates the globe. The vehicle's occupants are divided into a military ruling elite and their dishevelled captives. But now, 17 years after the train set out on its seemingly never-ending journey, the downtrodden, abused captives have had enough. Revolution is afoot. Bong Joon Ho's fantasy thriller, starring Chris Evans, Song Kang-ho, Tilda Swinton, Jamie Bell and Octavia Spencer.(n)

programme.title: The Keeping Room (2014)
programme.desc: Three women living on an isolated farm during the American Civil War must defend their home from renegade Union soldiers using a mission to track down deserters as cover for murder, rape and robbery. Western, starring Brit Marling, Hailee Steinfeld, Muna Otaru and Sam Worthington.(n)

programme.title: Makala (2017)
programme.desc: Emmanuel Gras's award-winning documentary about a young farmer from the Democratic Republic of the Congo who supports his family by making and selling charcoal. In Swahili and French.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Six members of the deaf community share their stories of life in lockdown during the coronavirus outbreak.(n)

programme.title: The Million Pound Note (1954)
programme.desc: An eccentric multimillionaire bets his equally rich brother that if a man is known to be wealthy, he can obtain everything he needs on credit. To put this theory to the test, they give a £1million note to a penniless seaman, but instruct him never to spend it. Comedy adapted from a story by Mark Twain, starring Gregory Peck, Jane Griffiths, Joyce Grenfell and Wilfrid Hyde White.(n)

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest (1960)
programme.desc: Legendary outlaw hero Robin Hood rallies his merry men to thwart a dastardly plan by the Sheriff of Nottingham to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury, who has denounced the sheriff's plot to confiscate another nobleman's land. Medieval adventure, starring Richard Greene, Peter Cushing, Niall MacGinnis, Richard Pasco, Sarah Branch and Oliver Reed.(n)

programme.title: Harry and the Hendersons (1987)
programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall.(n)

programme.title: Crack in the World (1965)
programme.desc: A dying scientist and his colleagues conduct experiments to harness the Earth's inner energy that seem set to end in disaster. Sci-fi thriller, with Dana Andrews, Janette Scott, Kieron Moore and Alexander Knox.(n)

programme.title: Super 8 (2011)
programme.desc: A boy and his friends sneak out at night to film an amateur zombie movie, only to witness a train derailment. As people start disappearing from their town and the military descends in force, they come to realise a dangerous alien was being transported on the train and is now on the loose. Sci-fi thriller, starring Joel Courtney and Elle Fanning.(n)

programme.title: Allied (2016)
programme.desc: A daring Canadian intelligence officer falls for a beautiful French resistance fighter during the Second World War. When they reunite in London, their relationship is tested by the pressures of conflict. Epic Robert Zemeckis thriller, starring Brad Pitt, Marion Cotillard and Jared Harris.(n)

programme.title: Jane Got a Gun (2015)
programme.desc: A rancher returns home badly wounded following a confrontation with a gang of outlaws. His wife realises the bandits will soon come looking for him, and is forced into an uneasy alliance with her former lover to defend her home and family from attack. Western, starring Natalie Portman and Ewan McGregor.(n)

programme.desc: Biopic of 1970s adult film star Linda Lovelace, contrasting her glamorous reputation following the release of groundbreaking adult movie Deep Throat - which made her an international star - to the dark account of exploitation and abuse she revealed to the world years later. Starring Amanda Seyfried, Peter Sarsgaard, Sharon Stone and Robert Patrick.(n)

programme.title: Tiger Bay (1959)
programme.desc: A Polish sailor flees Cardiff's docklands after murdering his girlfriend in a jealous rage, initially unaware that a 12-year-old tomboy has witnessed the crime and has taken his abandoned gun to show off to her friends. However, he comes back to abduct the girl in a bid to keep her quiet - but the pair form a friendship and she tries to help him escape the police. Thriller, starring Hayley Mills in her film debut alongside her father John, with Horst Buchholz.(n)

programme.title: Elephant Walk (1954)
programme.desc: A woman struggles to come to terms with her tea plantation owner husband's obsession with his dead father. Melodrama, starring Elizabeth Taylor, Peter Finch, Dana Andrews and Abraham Sofaer.(n)

programme.title: Freedom Radio (1940)

However, events take a sickening turn when his wife begins wholeheartedly espousing Hitler's views - prompting him to start broadcasting Allied propaganda from a secret transmitter. Second World War drama, starring Clive Brook, Diana Wynyard, Raymond Huntley and Derek Farr.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Voyage (1966)

Richard Fleischer's sci-fi adventure, starring Raquel Welch, Stephen Boyd, Donald Pleasence and Edmond O'Brien.(n)

Sci-fi drama, starring Amy Adams, Jeremy Renner and Forest Whitaker.(n)

When no evidence of wrongdoing is found, Jones is freed from custody, but the distraught father of one of the missing youngsters is not convinced of Jones' innocence, and kidnaps him in the hope of extracting a confession by force. programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Tucker acts like a lawyer in front of his father because he has forgotten to tell him he has dropped out of law school and works in TV. Meanwhile, Riley has a crush on a British guy, but Bonnie mistakenly thinks she has fallen for Danny. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle asks Peralta to be his best man, but as well as helping out with cake-tasting and choosing invitations, he must also persuade the groom to start standing up to Vivian before the big day. Holt helps Diaz make a sincere apology and Jeffords, Santiago and Gina embark on a tough new diet - but struggle to stick to it.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta enlists Jeffords to help him tackle a cold case that everyone thinks is unsolvable. Meanwhile, Santiago plans a romantic weekend away with her new boyfriend Teddy, and Gina and Diaz tell Boyle one of their best-kept secrets. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Melissa Fumero.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam worries about being 'hazed' by the older students when he goes to high school, but he soon earns their respect by body-slamming Barry. Erica and her friends talk about what they are going to put into their school-time capsule.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam works on a new persona that he hopes will make him seem cool on his first day at high school. Beverly gets her teaching certificate and aims to work as a substitute teacher at her children's school, but things go awry and the kids end up in a Saturday detention. But after Murray pays a visit to the school, everyone learns an important lesson. Tim Meadows and Allie Grant guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang become concerned when Sheldon's former admirer, Dr Ramona Nowitzki, resurfaces while Amy is away working at Princeton University. Jim Parsons stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy must decide whether or not to marry Sheldon and gives him an answer to his proposal. Howard and Bernadette struggle with some unexpected news. With guest stars Laurie Metcalf, Riki Lindhome and Stephen Hawking.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Marcel Somerville of Blazin' Squad and Love Island fame hosts tonight's dinner party, but misjudges his main course and comes in for some tough questions about his reality TV past.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle investigate a serial killer who poses a severe danger to the public. Captain Holt continues to spar with his rival, Commissioner Kelly. Jeffords must deal with some complications resulting from his recent news about his lieutenant's exam.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and his father deal with unsettled family business. Amy and Rosa work a high-profile case. Terry wants to join the NYPD band.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: At the start of the new term, Carli organises a fashion show that Simon is eager to participate in. Will tries to shake off his embarrassing reputation, while Jay continues to lie about his sexual experiences. Comedy, starring Emily Head, Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon goes to his first gig since seeing Take That at Wembley Arena, hoping to impress an attractive girl in the year below, but Will, Jay and Neil tag along and jeopardise his chances of wooing her. Comedy, starring Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison.(n)

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Comedy set against the Troubles in early-1990s Northern Ireland. The start of a new school term gets off to a bad start for Erin when she wakes to find her cousin reading her diary. But there is worse to come when Granda Joe announces a bomb has been found on the bridge. Saoirse-Monica Jackson stars.(n)

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Everyone is excited when Sister Michael announces that this year's school trip is to Paris, and Clare and James decide that they want to improve their French. Meanwhile at the Quinn house, Uncle Colm, Granda Joe's extremely boring brother, is visiting and the whole family are losing the will to live.(n)

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Jen has to handle a difficult builder, Roy loses £20, and Moss learns how to stand up for himself against a bunch of ruffians. Starring Chris O'Dowd, Katherine Parkinson, Richard Ayoade and Matt Berry.(n)

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Roy and Moss revel in their masculinity when they discover a new football website, but cannot keep up the charade for long. Jen has a date with a man who looks like a magician. Comedy, starring Chris O'Dowd, Katherine Parkinson and Richard Ayoade.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and her team help Shaun and Jannine Cartwright and their three sons transform their Blackburn home, which is full of clutter. They look to the 1950s for inspiration, creating a family playroom for the boys.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Duncan, who has always loved Fiats, bought his iconic 1967 Fiat 500 with the aim of restoring it in memory of his late wife. However, after a second family tragedy when his youngest son died, grieving Duncan struggled to find his motivation and the car sat rusting in a damp garage. While Fuzz Townshend puzzles over the Fiat's tiny engine, Tim Shaw goes into shock when the car's shell comes back from the acid dippers. The boys go on a road trip to Turin and, as always, it's a race against time to get the Italian job done.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The Sunbeam Alpine, a sleek two-seater convertible, was one of the most stylish British sports cars of the 1960s, and is now back in fashion and much sought after. Former mechanic and motor racing enthusiast Ray bought his Sunbeam Alpine over 20 years ago as a retirement project. Ray was always an active cyclist and skier, but after he suffered a brain injury six years ago he was left with severe disabilities. Knowing how much the car means to him, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend use all their motoring know-how and parts-blagging guile to get Ray's car back on the road.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Caterham 7 belonging to a former amateur racer, who had to focus on caring for his family after his wife was diagnosed with cancer.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in south-west London to help another two couples find their dream home. Long-distance partners Jason and Jaye are looking for a one-bed pad with outside space in Clapham or Brixton to finally bring them together, while Hannah and Frank want a family home before they get married - but have viewed nearly 50 properties without finding the one they want.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Cardiff to help another two couples find their dream home. Jo and Steve are relocating from Bristol to cut down on her commute to work, and Kirstie is on hand to provide them with advice on suitable properties for their needs. Phil accompanies Shauna and Todd, who admit their pricey taste in property is making it tough to find a family home.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Horton's passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carpenter Wes Leaver hosts the first party in east Kent, hoping his science theme will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize at the end of the week. However, the 34-year-old faces competition from estate agent Alexandra Austin, beautician Emma Phillips, dog-lover Sally Parry and fancy-dress enthusiast Stephen Drew.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Alexandra Austen hosts the second party of the week in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with pan-European cuisine. However, the meal gets off to a shaky start when Sally Parry is forced to confront her fear of beans, while Stephen Drew struggles with a goat's cheese parcel. Alexandra's decision to serve veal also proves controversial, and it soon becomes clear two of the guests are not enjoying each other's company.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beautician Emma Phillips hosts the third dinner party in east Kent. Her main course is described by one diner as a pet on a plate, and Alexandra Austin declares the menu to be rather odd. Meanwhile, Stephen Drew and Wes Leaver enjoy an impromptu beauty treatment together.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Sally Parry hosts the fourth dinner party in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with a pink-themed night, including posh pink food and table decorations. However, Sally and Emma have a heated argument, Alex misbehaves and Wes falls asleep.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant Stephen Drew hosts the final dinner party in east Kent, and has designed a menu that will allow him to spend time entertaining his guests. With an Absolutely Fabulous theme, a few of the diners are expecting a Mardi Gras vibe, but he has different ideas for the night - before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Leigh Osborne and Graham Voce want to convert and extend a landmark 150-year-old water tower in Kennington, south London, into a luxury home. Grade II-listed, derelict and with 6ft-thick walls, it's a huge challenge for the pair, who also plan to build two structures at the base - a lift shaft connected by a series of glass tunnels, and a modern living space. The result will be a four-bedroom property over nine floors, complete with a room at the top offering spectacular 360-degree views across the capital. Presented by Kevin McCloud.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Coventry are called to a pregnant woman with severe stomach pains, where a potentially life-threatening ectopic pregnancy is suspected.(n)

programme.title: Inside Birmingham Children's Hospital
programme.desc: Cameras follow three children facing very different challenges, including accident-prone four-year-old Finley who is putting first-time parents Mark and Nicola through their paces as he is treated for a cut on his forehead. There's also a profile of 10-year-old Louie, who faces the prospect of major heart surgery, while his parents Kerry and Damien struggle to hide their anxieties about the surgery.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary following the emergency services in Wiltshire, focusing on car crimes committed by young drivers. PC Nick Denning is dispatched in pursuit of a 'pool car' - a cheap, unregistered car shared by several friends - which is currently being driven at breakneck speed around residential streets. Other officers pursue suspected joyriders, and are shocked to find the driver is only 14.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Michelle, who's 54, is airlifted to St George's after being involved in a car crash with her husband Paul in Surrey. She was given a blood transfusion at the scene and doctors work quickly to get her to CT to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Cameras also follow Derren, 41, who has fractured his ankle while trampolining with his 12-year-old daughter, Macie.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Coventry are called to a pregnant woman with severe stomach pains, where a potentially life-threatening ectopic pregnancy is suspected.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary following the emergency services in Wiltshire, focusing on car crimes committed by young drivers. PC Nick Denning is dispatched in pursuit of a 'pool car' - a cheap, unregistered car shared by several friends - which is currently being driven at breakneck speed around residential streets. Other officers pursue suspected joyriders, and are shocked to find the driver is only 14.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priest makes a valiant effort to give up cigarettes for Lent, inspired by a challenge from Dick Byrne, his opposite number over on Rugged Island, but soon finds he is not cut out for a life of self-denial. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp competes in the Something New from Something Old competition at the Royal Wales Show, creating vintage paper jewellery, but finds herself up against stiff competition.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Robert and Diana Tayler search for a home in the Dordogne region of south-west France. The couple have already sold their Norfolk house, so are eager to find somewhere new to live as soon as possible.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul prepare for their biggest chateau booking ever as they host American veterans for the 75th anniversary of D-Day. Mariam and Johnno transform their chateau's grand salon by installing reproduction Parquet de Versailles flooring and hand-painted murals. Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a couple building a traditional Scottish cottage in the Firth of Forth, whose ambitious plans have been jeopardised by bad weather.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brigitte Tee, who runs four-star B&B Gorse Meadow in the New Forest, hosts the first round of the competition. Her guests are initially charmed by her eccentric personality, but the discovery of a bathroom window in need of repair and another room containing ladybirds, cobwebs and dust soon takes the shine off their stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundell's four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the B&B three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves north to Blackpool and the three-star Vidella B&B run by John Downer and Neil Williams. The pair rig Blackpool-hater Alistair Mundell's room with practical jokes, and take their guests to Funny Girls drag cabaret bar, where Mark Crib confesses he has never had such a surreal time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Crib welcomes the group to the Urban Beach in Bournemouth, Dorset, for the week's final B&B visit. The boutique establishment features chic rooms with deluxe bathrooms, and the guests approve of the afternoon's activity - a speedboat ride along the Jurassic coast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Brigitte Tee is upset to discover she has been given only one point for cleanliness and a heated exchange results in a walk-out, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.sub-title: Forth and Clyde Canal and the Union Canal
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel along the Forth and Clyde Canal and the Union Canal in a coast-to-coast adventure from Edinburgh to Glasgow, taking in Scotland's highest aqueduct and longest canal tunnel, as well some striking examples of 21st-century engineering and monumental sculptures.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul take on a massive gardening project, expanding a lake and creating an al fresco dining area. Mariam and Johnno launch their chateau as an events venue.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Audrey and Jeff Lovelock, who dream of living in one of west London's most exclusive areas. However, the only way they can afford it is to go underground. Their plan is to transform a listed Edwardian artist's studio - along with 5,000 sq ft of derelict basement - into a bespoke subterranean home, complete with supersize kitchen, mezzanine sitting room, four en-suite bedrooms, a gym, cinema, steam room and wine cellar. As if the task was not tricky enough, they are doing it without proper architects' drawings and Audrey is project managing - and the difficulties begin almost from the start.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Treasure Hunters
programme.sub-title: Hitler's Secret Obsession: Nazi Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: A team of experts attempts to track down art looted from across Europe under Hitler's orders, with items worth billions still missing to this day. In Austria, Conor Woodman explores the salt mine where Allied soldiers recovered the majority of Hitler's stolen art in 1945, but meets local treasure hunters and investigative journalists who are convinced some of it was smuggled out and is today hidden in nearby homes and mountain hideouts.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures
programme.desc: A look at the Battle of the Bulge, which began in Decmeber 1944 when Hitler went on the counteroffensive in the Ardennes having secretly assembled a vast strike force.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Phil Wang taking on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Father Jack is hauled off to the local old priests' home to be treated for a strange medical complaint. He is replaced by a younger man - who proceeds to drive everyone crazy by playing jungle music at ear-splitting volume for hours on end. Dermot Morgan and Frank Kelly star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate.(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates another look back at TV shows from the 1970s, focusing on the comedies, documentaries and public information films that made people so anxious 40-plus years ago. Some of the famous shows covered include Man Alive and Brass Tacks, as well as The Goodies taking on the police and June Whitfield burning her bra. The show features first-hand accounts from the people who were making telly at the time, including Barry Cryer, Janet Street-Porter and Bill Oddie.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Phil Wang taking on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Israel to find out about the mystery ingredient that makes icing sugar so light, fluffy and powdery, while Jimmy Doherty is in Italy to investigate the difference between risotto and long-grain types of rice. Matt Tebbutt discovers that one sandwich maker has come up with an innovative - and surprising - way of putting waste bread to good use.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp shows viewers some quirky ways to dress a dining table, and reveals how to make a paper napkin rose and a cupcake bouquet. The presenter also demonstrates a recipe for chocolate bowls.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: A Derbyshire couple go searching the French region of Aude for a home where they can start married life. But while Faye has her heart set on a character property, Philip likes modern clean lines. With £150,000, can Sara Damergi find something they agree on?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to the Cotswolds to see who can create the most profit on two old lamps, and also take a spin in a Devonshire fire engine.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien creates table lamps from old theatre lights and Henry Cole finds a treat for restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul take on a massive gardening project, expanding a lake and creating an al fresco dining area. Mariam and Johnno launch their chateau as an events venue.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cambridgeshire to meet self-builders Sarah and Rob, who are aiming to construct a family home in the village where they first met as children.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the B&B competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park B&B in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.sub-title: London's Lost Route to the Sea
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go in search of the lost route between London and the sea, which has not been fully open for more than 200 years and requires four different boats to traverse now. They leave the Thames at Teddington and travel through the Surrey and Sussex countryside before arriving at Guildford, and a canal-side theatre where they have both regularly performed. The final leg of the trip brings them to the River Arun before reaching the English Channel.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Mariam and Johnno restore their chateau's original bread oven for a sourdough weekend. Angel goes bargain hunting to find a unique radiator cover for the chateau's boot room, while Emma and Paul complete the final room in the west wing and theme it after the renowned artist Alphonse Mucha.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets artists Indi and Rebecca, who are planning to construct a modern, larch-clad home on the Isle of Skye, with a second hand-crafted building alongside to use as their studio, all on a tight budget of £150,000. They have been saving for years, but making it happen will mean taking on the savage weather, not to mention the sceptical locals - and as if that weren't enough, most of the work is being done by one man, local builder Donald.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Mohammed, 64, who has been hit by a pizza delivery motorbike. Consultant Will is concerned that he may have a pelvic fracture, which could seriously affect his mobility and, if it hits a major artery, can be life threatening. There's also a profile of John, a 90-year-old who comes to St George's in south west London with his wife Iris and daughter Joan after his GP picked up on his abnormally low heart rate.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Coventry are called to a pregnant woman with severe stomach pains, where a potentially life-threatening ectopic pregnancy is suspected.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: The Hurting
programme.desc: Clip show featuring the voice of Jake Yapp, as home videos reveal the dumbest things attempted by adults, kids and even animals.(n)

programme.title: The Hurting
programme.desc: Clip show featuring the voice of Jake Yapp, as home videos reveal the dumbest things attempted by adults, kids and even animals.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: Rick's barge trip brings him to Castelnaudary, where he visits the canteen of the French Foreign Legion, prepares a spicy tagine with couscous and enjoys a three-course lunch consisting of roast leg of veal with potato gratin dauphinioise, goats' cheese and strawberries.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food starts to smell the salty Mediterranean sea on the warm breeze and serves freshly grilled sardines with salad and cold rose wine for the boat crew as they reach Narbonne. Meanwhile, Louis, the chef onboard Anjodi, rustles up seafood with squid ink pasta.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie hits San Antonio to sample the finest Tex-Mex food and a gigantic chicken fried steak - with no chicken. He also tests whether he has the moves for TV show Ninja Warrior.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie gets a taste of Cuba in Miami's fresh seafood, sets his sights at a shooting range, and heads cross-state to attempt a huge surf & turf challenge created just for him.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Stoke strongman Eddie Hall visits cosmopolitan Queens, NY, to make a solo attempt at a huge Greek kebab banquet, sample the best Chinese dumplings and try his hand at baseball.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Norway to find out how a selection of cars fare when taking part in Winter Olympic events. Their experiments include firing a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump, tackling the biathlon with 4x4s, staging a game of motorised ice hockey and timing the Jaguar XK in a speed-skating race.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are challenged to build a bridge over the River Kwai in Thailand. In order to do that, however, they have to drive across Burma, a country that has been largely closed to Westerners for more than 40 years, and must make the trip in three lorries, each bought sight unseen from the internet on a limited budget. programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Documentary examining three landscapes in northern Scandinavia. In the mountainous highlands, cameras capture two endangered predators relying on a plentiful supply of lemmings.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Deep in the forest, a female bear and three cubs emerge in the sunshine after hibernation. The first priority for the mother is to find food - and quickly, or her cubs will starve.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Far north in Svalbard, the polar bear depends on the ice to hunt seals. But with the ice receding, it has been forced to turn its attentions to a larger, trickier prey - the walrus.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the connections between the wildlife, landscape and people of the Arabian peninsula. The first edition features animals including oryxes, jerboas, horned vipers and scorpions, and a journey with the Bedouin - camel-riding nomads who are the only humans to have mastered the art of survival in the desert. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Exploring the mountains of Dhofar in Oman, where the annual Indian Ocean monsoon has helped create a lost world of waterfalls and cloud forests filled with fascinating wildlife. The programme shows green sea turtles coming ashore in their thousands, shadowed by egg-stealing foxes, as well as footage of striped hyenas doing battle with Arabian wolves. Plus, a glimpse into the lives of chameleons, honey badgers and the elusive Arabian leopard.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: The changes that have swept across the Arabian peninsula since the discovery of oil. Camel racing has become a hi-tech sport and the region's animals now live alongside a very modern society, but technology is also being used to protect nature. The programme reveals how dugongs are fitted with satellite transmitters and hunting falcons chase down radio-controlled planes, and follows the construction of the world's first carbon-neutral city. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: Morecambe & Wise in America
programme.desc: Eric and Ern are invited back onto The Ed Sullivan Show on the same night as the Beatles, giving them an audience of more than 70 million viewers, and are also offered a three-film deal in Britain that could be their fast track to Hollywood. Featuring performances never seen before by British viewers, and interviews with many of the artists who worked with them, including Diana Rigg, Glenda Jackson and Nicholas Parsons. Presented by Jonathan Ross.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Presuming Rene is dead, Edith hunts for his will and a new husband. Meanwhile, the airmen pose as nuns and Herr Flick enlists Lt Gruber's artistic expertise in distinguishing a real painting from two forgeries. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The British airmen are forced to return to their hideout at Rene's cafe following a bungled escape attempt, and Edith continues to search for a new husband. Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera star.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The British send their finest French-speaking agent to say 'good moaning' to Rene and his colleagues and, dressed as a pantomime cow, the two airmen narrowly avoid being milked before going to a landing field to pick up their plane home.(n)

programme.desc: Bren has a bad day - a convict escapes in the fog on his way to Strangeways, the blood donor van turns up at the factory, and Tony's holiday photos cause upset. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Andrew Dunn.(n)

programme.desc: The staff have a bet on Bren's chances of romance with Tony, but the hapless cook's past finally catches up with her and threatens to ruin any prospects of romance. Comedy, written by and starring Victoria Wood, with Thelma Barlow, Andrew Dunn, Celia Imrie and Shobna Gulati.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough shines the spotlight on some of nature's evolutionary anomalies and how these curious animals continue to baffle and fascinate. In this edition, he examines the unusual ways in which Komodo dragons and aphids reproduce.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the emperor penguin and the wood frog, both of which survive in conditions so cold they seem to defy the laws of nature. The emperor is the only penguin able to breed on Antarctic sea ice during winter, while the wood frog can survive up to 65 per cent of its body mass freezing during its hibernation.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, which have both developed incredible adaptations to help them navigate at night.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a look at gorillas and vampire bats, two species that have unfairly gained unpleasant reputation within the animal kingdom.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey explains how writing a biography of his ancestor John, the first Duke of Marlborough, changed the way Winston Churchill was seen by the public and politicians, and so paved the way for his return to power. The first volume, published in 1933, reinforced the perception of him as a man past his time. But it was the second instalment, depicting John Churchill's march to the Danube and victory at Blenheim, which began to mark out Winston as the only leading politician who truly knew war.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates historical evidence of the story of Jesus, examining his execution and his relationship with Mary Magdalene.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most incredible metropolitan railways, and how they help people solve distinctly urban problems. Including a project to upgrade New York's transit system.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Royal William Yard in Plymouth, where they are given the chance to cast their eyes over a number of potentially valuable items. On the waterfront, militaria expert Graham Lay has doubts about a pair of doorknobs thought to have belonged to Admiral Horatio Nelson. The team is impressed by a chess set made from parts of the aircraft carrier HMS Ark Royal, and a psychedelic painting of a band that performed at Woodstock intrigues Clive Stewart-Lockhart. Plus, jewellery specialist John Benjamin marvels at a diamond necklace the length of a skipping rope, donated to the city of Plymouth by Lady Astor.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Cillian Murphy narrates a documentary examining the lives of animals and people pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic, from the world's most powerful currents, ferocious hurricanes and huge chains of undersea volcanoes. The first episode looks at the influence of the Gulf Stream, with whales, giant turtles and jellyfish riding the huge current that becomes a migration super-highway and helps warm northern Europe, while fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to exploit its natural fertility.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of omnivores, nature's generalists, revealing their myriad talents in the wild and how some species have adapted to man's encroachment into their habitats by making their homes in cities and towns.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Engineering breakthroughs that allow trains to go faster than ever, including technology that allows the navigation of the winding mountains of Spain at speed.(n)

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay travels to the El Greco restaurant in Austin, Texas, where the owners have resorted to using a microwave to prepare their 'authentic' Greek dishes. Gordon faces the difficult task of uniting a squabbling family before tackling the bigger problem of a business on the brink of collapse.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

Broadcaster Jeremy Paxman, comedian Joe Wilkinson, actress Sally Lindsay and reality star Georgia Toffolo make characterful biscuits for their Signature challenge, followed by a Technical that leaves them nowhere to hide, and finally they make a cake in the shape of their favourite hobby. They're baking to inspire viewers to do the same and raise money for Stand Up to Cancer.(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Online relationship guru Breeny spends her time advising others on their love lives, but has yet to find 'The One' herself. She wants a man with spirit - something that 6ft personal trainer Bavoray is not short of. Mum of two Lisa lost the love of her life three years ago and is now looking for someone to spend the future with - crucially someone who isn't bald. Her date is businessman Steve McCool. But will his full head of hair be enough in the end? Make-up artist Kiera is paired up with Jack, an Essex builder with an extremely thorough pre-date beauty regime.(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Levison Wood reaches the final stage of his epic 650-mile journey, following the annual migration of Botswana's 130,000 elephants. Together with his guide Kane, Lev follows the elephants deep into the heart of the Okavango Delta, a vast wetland teaming with wildlife where every year more than 40,000 elephants gather to feed. Lev and Kane narrowly avoid a pride of lions who have brought down a male buffalo and watch as they move in for the kill, but are forced to wade through wide channels where crocodiles, hippos and snakes lurk.(n)

Cornish vet Emily attempts a life-threatening giraffe relocation, while marine biologist Alison finds a new way of dealing with plastic pollution on the Western Cape.(n)
